Great opportunity to work with talented people from around the world. The salary and benefits are strong overall, though they can vary depending on location. Great People and Benefits, Challenging Career Progression

Exposure to real-time projects with ample learning opportunities. Encourages ownership and responsibility, even at early career stages. Supportive team culture â colleagues and managers are approachable and helpful. Leadership is accessible and open to ideas and feedback. Good Exposure to Projects, People, and Processes
Great benefits, decent work/life balace, lots of opportunity for growth and new roles Typical Giant Company
Office infrastructure is good. Some benefits, like the car lease policy, and the Cafeteria Food is good Full of politics, No growth, WLB is a challenge

The company has a fantastic, people-centered culture. Colleagues are kind, and thereâs a clear effort to make employees feel valued. Internal events foster collaboration and connection, and there are numerous opportunities to grow into diverse roles. Thereâs a strong commitment to developing talent from within, which creates a sense of long-term investment in employees. Supportive Culture with Internal Growth â...

Supportive and friendly coworkers â¢ Minimal micromanagement â¢ Low-stress workload compared to similar roles â¢ Consistent salary increases â¢ True 9-to-5 hours with little overtime Low-Stress Environment with Friendly Team, But Lacks Clear Direction

evidence.role: Current employee - Associate Solution Support Engineer
evidence.text: - Feeling of being forced into the office for work that can be done easily at home - Board doesn't really listen to employees even though they say they do Great company to work for

Hard to negotiate a pay raise Low salary

Little to no room for advancement. Often role I cherry picked for a select individual, yet interviews still happed as if thereâs a chance for you. Leadership constantly changes objectives and direction before the first objective is halfway completed. The hype is not real
Too many layoffs. The role is not valued and appreciated by the leadership. SAP-UAD
No growth opportunity if you wanted to move up Ok

Constant restructuring. Zero career progression. Below inflation pay increases. No chance of promotion or advancement. Company thinks AI will save it. Stupid return to office policy. Zero career or salary progression
Not much growth and salary increment. HR sucks. Not for growth or culture
Not a very close knit community, many remote workers which decreases interaction Great environment
too many changes in a short period of time not recommended

*The unspoken rule you need to give â200%â to the company which sets the expectation of constant overwork and intense pressure leading to big stress. *The company culture is toxic and broken. Instead of collaboration, people compete internally, undermining each other to get ahead. *The company hides behind flashy marketing and âcool brandingâ, but the reality inside is completely different â rotten to the...

Challenging environment where we are something left to our own devices (which can sometimes be positive of course). It's not easy to gain respect and trust from older colleagues who have way more experience and that can also bring its challenges. Targets are quite high and also include some exceptions and rules which makes it harder to succeed Great company to evolve in
Pay is not so good compared to the top competitors sometimes Great company for social life
south india politics and manager are biased and very strict good company but south politics
politics, must know the management politics

Toxic managing environment. No pay raises for the year on a global scale even though stock price was soaring and employees were making great efforts through RTO mandates, HPOM model alignment and embedding AI into products. A new performance review program introduced in order to micromanage more efficiently and withhold raises/bonuses based on manager discretion. Had some good diversity programs but they were cut du...

evidence.role: Current employee - Senior Industry Account Executive
evidence.text: Constant re-orgs and layoffs, lack of resources, culture of fear due to constant change. Paralysis. Too much change caused paralysis and toxic culture
No work/life management. Managers have no clue what they are doing. A lot of sexism. Favoritism. Not a good place to work
